campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships in the city. The council also discussed extending a temporary local ordinance and moving forward with plans for a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance number 2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17 project moving forward
Grant applications being submitted for bike/pedestrian planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or law. After listening to residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and skip the usual first step of the approval process so they could vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance being fast-tracked for approval
Council skipped first reading to speed up adoption
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February 2026. The council also authorized several construction and professional service contracts, though the agenda text cuts off before providing full details on what specific projects or services these contracts cover.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes (both public and closed session)
February 2026 monthly financial and investment report
Construction service contracts with QLM, Inc. and another contractor
Professional services agreement (3-year term)
Infrastructure improvements resolution

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting on April 22, 2026 to organize upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 28 meeting, decided which items needed specific time slots, and started planning the May 5 meeting agenda. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Set time slots for specific agenda items to keep meetings on track
Reviewed public records from April 9-16, 2026
Started planning the May 5, 2026 council meeting
Addressed any special waiver requirements for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction projects and improvements the city wants to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and fixing city infrastructure
Official recognition of Teen Month
Official recognition of National Volunteer Week
Planning session on capital improvement projects

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting at 6 PM to discuss confidential matters (details not publicly disclosed), then reconvened at 7 PM for a regular public meeting that included a flag salute and recognitions for community winners or honorees.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (specific topics not disclosed to public)
Recognition and awards for community winners
Regular council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 21, 2026 to discuss two pending lawsuits that could cost the city money, and to recognize the work of the Administrative Services Department's budget and finance teams. The meeting also included an open comment period where residents could speak to the council about any issues they wanted to raise.
keyTopics: Two lawsuits with potential financial risk to the city
Recognition of city budget and finance staff
Public comment period for resident concerns

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large decorative signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters through 2029. One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (2026-2029)
Staff withdrew one agenda item from consideration

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to vote on several financial and development matters. The council approved the reissuance of a tax responsibility act, reviewed the city's development budget for the next fiscal year, and updated various user fees. The council also heard a presentation about efforts to reduce homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development budget approval
Updates to city user fees
Santa Clara County homelessness reduction initiative
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that was already approved for the property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow protected trees to be cut down on that lot.
keyTopics: 751 Waverley Street tree removal permit under review
Request to reconsider an already-approved permit decision
Protected trees at stake
Planning Director's authority to revisit the approval
